# Work Packages: WSD Generator

**Inputs**: Design documents from `/kitty-specs/017-wsd-generator-cross-validator/`
**Prerequisites**: plan.md (required), spec.md (user stories), research.md, data-model.md, quickstart.md
**Tests**: Tests are included as they are integral to verifying roundtrip fidelity (core acceptance criterion).

**Organization**: Fine-grained subtasks (`Txxx`) roll up into work packages (`WPxx`). Each work package must be independently deliverable and testable.

**Prompt Files**: Each work package references a matching prompt file in `/tasks/` generated by `/spec-kitty.tasks`.

## Subtask Format: `[Txxx] [P?] Description`
- **[P]** indicates the subtask can proceed in parallel (different files/components).
- Include precise file paths or modules.

## Path Conventions
- **Source**: `src/Frank.Statecharts/Wsd/`
- **Tests**: `test/Frank.Statecharts.Tests/Wsd/`

---

## Work Package WP01: WSD Serializer (Priority: P0)

**Goal**: Implement the `Wsd.Serializer` module that converts a `Diagram` AST into syntactically valid WSD text with Unix line endings.
**Independent Test**: Construct a `Diagram` AST programmatically, serialize it, and verify the output matches expected WSD text. Parse the output through `parseWsd` and confirm zero errors.
**Prompt**: `/tasks/WP01-wsd-serializer.md`
**Estimated Prompt Size**: ~350 lines

### Included Subtasks
- [x] T001 [P] Create `Wsd/Serializer.fs` module skeleton with `serialize`, `needsQuoting`, `quoteName` function signatures
- [x] T002 Implement `needsQuoting` ŌĆö returns true for names with non-identifier characters (spaces, hyphens, special chars)
- [x] T003 Implement `quoteName` ŌĆö wraps name in double quotes if `needsQuoting`, escaping internal quotes
- [x] T004 Implement `serialize` ŌĆö emits title, participant declarations (initial state first), blank line separator, messages with `->` arrows, note annotations with `[guard: key=value]` syntax, Unix `\n` line endings
- [x] T005 Add `Wsd/Serializer.fs` to `Frank.Statecharts.fsproj` compile items after `Wsd/Parser.fs`
- [x] T006 [P] Create `Wsd/SerializerTests.fs` in test project with unit tests covering title emission, participant ordering, message formatting, guard note formatting, quoting, and edge cases (empty diagram, single participant, self-message)

### Implementation Notes
- Serializer depends only on `Wsd.Types` (no dependency on Generator or Parser at runtime)
- Use `System.Text.StringBuilder` for efficient text construction
- All `SourcePosition` fields in generated AST nodes use `{ Line = 0; Column = 0 }` (synthetic position convention)
- File order in `.fsproj`: `Wsd/Types.fs` -> `Wsd/Lexer.fs` -> `Wsd/GuardParser.fs` -> `Wsd/Parser.fs` -> `Wsd/Serializer.fs`

### Parallel Opportunities
- T001-T005 (source) and T006 (tests) can proceed in parallel since tests only depend on the module's public API signatures.
- WP01 and WP02 can proceed in parallel per the plan's dependency graph (both depend only on existing `Wsd.Types`).

### Dependencies
- None (starting package). Depends only on existing `Wsd/Types.fs`.

### Risks & Mitigations
- Serializer output must be parseable by the existing WSD parser -> validate via `parseWsd` in SerializerTests.
- Edge cases with quoting logic (embedded quotes, Unicode) -> cover in tests.

---

## Work Package WP02: WSD Generator (Priority: P0)

**Goal**: Implement the `Wsd.Generator` module that converts `StateMachineMetadata` into a `Diagram` AST by extracting states from `StateHandlerMap`, transitions from handler method names, and guards via reflection on the boxed `Machine: obj`.
**Independent Test**: Construct a `StateMachineMetadata` with known states and transitions, call `generate`, and verify the resulting `Diagram` AST contains the expected participants, messages, and guard annotations.
**Prompt**: `/tasks/WP02-wsd-generator.md`
**Estimated Prompt Size**: ~450 lines

### Included Subtasks
- [x] T007 Create `Wsd/Generator.fs` module with `GeneratorError` DU, `GenerateOptions` record, and `generate` function signature
- [x] T008 Implement state discovery ŌĆö extract participant names from `StateHandlerMap` keys, order initial state first
- [x] T009 Implement transition discovery ŌĆö emit `MessageElement` for each (state, httpMethod) pair from `StateHandlerMap` values
- [x] T010 Implement guard extraction via reflection ŌĆö unbox `Machine: obj`, check if generic type is `StateMachine<_,_,_>`, extract `Guards` field, emit `NoteElement` with `[guard: name=*]` syntax
- [x] T011 Add `Wsd/Generator.fs` to `Frank.Statecharts.fsproj` compile items after `Wsd/Serializer.fs`
- [x] T012 [P] Create `Wsd/GeneratorTests.fs` in test project with unit tests covering happy path (3-state turnstile), single state, empty handlers, guard emission, unrecognized machine type error, and special character state names

### Implementation Notes
- Generator depends on `Wsd.Types` and `StateMachineMetadata` (from `StatefulResourceBuilder.fs`)
- The `generate` function returns `Result<Diagram, GeneratorError>` ŌĆö no exceptions for expected failures
- Guard annotations are machine-wide (not per-transition): emitted as `note over <initialState>` after participant declarations
- File order in `.fsproj`: `...Parser.fs` -> `Wsd/Serializer.fs` -> `Wsd/Generator.fs`
- Tests need to construct `StateMachineMetadata` instances ŌĆö use helper functions that create minimal valid metadata

### Parallel Opportunities
- T007-T011 (source) and T012 (tests) can proceed in parallel.
- WP02 can proceed in parallel with WP01 since both depend only on `Wsd.Types`.

### Dependencies
- None (can run in parallel with WP01). Both WP01 and WP02 depend only on existing `Wsd/Types.fs`.

### Risks & Mitigations
- Reflection on boxed generic type may be fragile -> use `FSharp.Reflection` APIs and test across all TFMs.
- `Transition` function is opaque -> document limitation; generator shows state capabilities, not full transition graph.
- Constructing test `StateMachineMetadata` requires creating closures for `GetCurrentStateKey`, `EvaluateGuards`, `ExecuteTransition` -> use stub implementations that return defaults.

---

## Work Package WP03: Roundtrip Tests and Integration (Priority: P1) -- MVP

**Goal**: Implement parse-generate-reparse roundtrip tests that verify the full pipeline: generate WSD from metadata, parse it back, and confirm AST equivalence. Add test files to `.fsproj`, verify build across all TFMs, and validate the end-to-end pipeline matches spec acceptance criteria.
**Independent Test**: Generate WSD from a turnstile `StateMachineMetadata`, parse the output via `parseWsd`, verify zero parse errors, and confirm participant/message/guard structural equivalence.
**Prompt**: `/tasks/WP03-roundtrip-tests-integration.md`
**Estimated Prompt Size**: ~350 lines

### Included Subtasks
- [x] T013 Create `Wsd/GeneratorRoundTripTests.fs` in test project with roundtrip test infrastructure (helper functions for AST comparison)
- [x] T014 Implement turnstile roundtrip test: construct 3-state metadata, generate WSD via `Generator.generate` + `Serializer.serialize`, parse back via `parseWsd`, verify participant names, message labels, and title match
- [x] T015 Implement edge case roundtrip tests: single state (no transitions), self-transition, state names with special characters (quoting), metadata with guards (verify guard annotations survive roundtrip)
- [x] T016 Add `Wsd/SerializerTests.fs`, `Wsd/GeneratorTests.fs`, `Wsd/GeneratorRoundTripTests.fs` to test project `.fsproj` compile items (before `Program.fs`)
- [x] T017 Verify `dotnet build src/Frank.Statecharts/Frank.Statecharts.fsproj` succeeds for all TFMs (net8.0, net9.0, net10.0) and `dotnet test test/Frank.Statecharts.Tests/` passes all tests including new ones

### Implementation Notes
- Roundtrip tests use `Generator.generate` + `Serializer.serialize` + `Parser.parseWsd` ŌĆö the full pipeline
- AST comparison focuses on semantic equivalence: same participant names (in order), same message sender/receiver/label pairs, same guard annotation pairs
- Cosmetic differences (whitespace, exact formatting) are acceptable per spec
- The `LexerTests.fs` entry is NOT in `.fsproj` (Lexer tests are in `Wsd/LexerTests.fs` if they exist, but check current state). Only add test files that actually exist.

### Parallel Opportunities
- None within this WP (tests depend on both Generator and Serializer being complete).

### Dependencies
- Depends on WP01 (Serializer) and WP02 (Generator).

### Risks & Mitigations
- Guard roundtrip may not be exact (generator emits `name=*`, parser reads `name=*`) -> test that the key and wildcard value survive roundtrip.
- Parser may emit warnings for generated output (e.g., implicit participants) -> ensure generator uses explicit `ParticipantDecl` elements.

---

## Dependency & Execution Summary

- **Sequence**: WP01 and WP02 can run in parallel (Phase 1a/1b) -> WP03 depends on both (Phase 2).
- **Parallelization**: WP01 (Serializer) and WP02 (Generator) are fully independent and can be assigned to different agents simultaneously.
- **MVP Scope**: All three work packages constitute MVP. WP01 + WP02 deliver the core modules; WP03 validates the end-to-end pipeline and matches spec acceptance criteria (SC-001, SC-002).

```
WP01 (Serializer) ŌöĆŌöĆŌöÉ
                     Ōö£ŌöĆŌöĆ> WP03 (Roundtrip + Integration)
WP02 (Generator) ŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöś
```
